text: Starting at 2026-05-16T08:41:12, Suricata IDS detected 2 alert(s) from internal host 10.10.24.57 communicating with external IP(s) 185.193.126.44 on port(s) 443. Signature "ET MALWARE Possible C2 Beaconing Activity" was triggered, indicating the host may be infected with malware that is periodically checking in with a command-and-control server (T1071.001 (Application Layer Protocol: Web Protocols)). Signature "ET TROJAN Suspicious Periodic HTTPS Beacon" was triggered, indicating the host may be infected with malware that is periodically checking in with a command-and-control server (T1071.001 (Application Layer Protocol: Web Protocols)). Alert severity is critical (level 1), indicating high-confidence malicious activity. DNS activity from 10.10.24.57 included 2 query/queries:   • DNS TXT query for "ajd82j3k2k2k.cmd-sync-storage.com" TXT record responses containing base64-encoded data suggest DNS tunneling for data exfiltration or C2 (T1071.004 (Application Layer Protocol: DNS)).   • DNS TXT query for "ajd82j3k2k2k.cmd-sync-storage.com" Network flow data shows 1244 bytes sent to the external server and 1987 bytes received, consistent with periodic small check-in beacons rather than large data transfers. The combination of C2 beaconing alerts, suspicious DNS queries, and regular outbound connections from 10.10.24.57 strongly suggests this host is compromised and under active remote control.
